fields.summary: Expose per-application over-allocation info in the Resource Manager
[Umbrella] Schedule containers based on utilization of currently allocated containers

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.

fields.description: When enabling over-allocation of nodes, the resources in the cluster change. We need to surface this information for users to understand these changes.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

[~haibochen] : Couple of comments:

 

1. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ApplicationResourceUsageReport#newInstance:
 I think it's somewhat hard to read this method because it contains too many parameters.
 Since this method has only two calls, I think it would be cleaner to use a builder here if it's not a big deal to refactor.

2. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.recovery.records.ApplicationAttemptStateData#newInstance
 Same as 1.)

3. org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/applicationhistoryservice/ApplicationHistoryManagerOnTimelineStore.java:332
 I would create 3 methods here, namely: createGuaranteedResourceSecondsMap, createPreemptedResoureSecondsMap and createOpportunisticResourceSecondsMap.
 These methods could extract the memorySeconds / vCoreSeconds values from the entityInfo and put them to a new map and return it.
 This way, the if clause would be far more readable, e.g. :
{code:java}
if (entityInfo.containsKey(
 ApplicationMetricsConstants.APP_GUARANTEED_CPU_METRICS)) {
 appResources = ApplicationResourceUsageReport
 .newInstance(0, 0, null, null, null,
 createGuaranteedResourceSecondsMap(entityInfo), 0, 0,
 createPreemptedResoureSecondsMap(entityInfo), null,
 createOpportunisticResourceSecondsMap(entityInfo));
}{code}
4. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.recovery.TestZKRMStateStore#createFinishedAttempt
 Here, I would use a similar approach like in 3.), so creating 3 separate methods that gives back the guaranteed / preempted / opportunistic resource seconds maps, respectively.

5. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.TestContainerResourceUsage:
 It seems the System.out in line 126 is left there accidentally.

6. There is one TODO item in org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/resourcemanager/TestContainerResourceUsage.java:468, does it still apply?

Thanks [~snemeth] for the review! For 1) and 2), given the patch is already very large and there are only 1 or 2 constructors, I think we can leave the refactoring to another jira.

For 4), instead of adding three more methods, I used anonymous HashMap class to make it more readable.  3), 5) and 6) are all addressed accordingly. Thanks again for the review.

[~haibochen] : Couple of comments:

 

1. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.ApplicationResourceUsageReport#newInstance:
 I think it's somewhat hard to read this method because it contains too many parameters.
 Since this method has only two calls, I think it would be cleaner to use a builder here if it's not a big deal to refactor.

2. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.recovery.records.ApplicationAttemptStateData#newInstance
 Same as 1.)

3. org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/applicationhistoryservice/ApplicationHistoryManagerOnTimelineStore.java:332
 I would create 3 methods here, namely: createGuaranteedResourceSecondsMap, createPreemptedResoureSecondsMap and createOpportunisticResourceSecondsMap.
 These methods could extract the memorySeconds / vCoreSeconds values from the entityInfo and put them to a new map and return it.
 This way, the if clause would be far more readable, e.g. :
{code:java}
if (entityInfo.containsKey(
 ApplicationMetricsConstants.APP_GUARANTEED_CPU_METRICS)) {
 appResources = ApplicationResourceUsageReport
 .newInstance(0, 0, null, null, null,
 createGuaranteedResourceSecondsMap(entityInfo), 0, 0,
 createPreemptedResoureSecondsMap(entityInfo), null,
 createOpportunisticResourceSecondsMap(entityInfo));
}{code}
4. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.recovery.TestZKRMStateStore#createFinishedAttempt
 Here, I would use a similar approach like in 3.), so creating 3 separate methods that gives back the guaranteed / preempted / opportunistic resource seconds maps, respectively.

5. org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.TestContainerResourceUsage:
 It seems the System.out in line 126 is left there accidentally.

6. There is one TODO item in org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/resourcemanager/TestContainerResourceUsage.java:468, does it still apply?

Thanks [~snemeth] for the review! For 1) and 2), given the patch is already very large and there are only 1 or 2 constructors, I think we can leave the refactoring to another jira.

For 4), instead of adding three more methods, I used anonymous HashMap class to make it more readable.  3), 5) and 6) are all addressed accordingly. Thanks again for the review.
